features.title: FastAPI REST API with CRUD endpoints
features.description: Projects, features, tasks, gates, approvals, dispatch, dashboard JSON API — 16 endpoints total
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints return correct status codes
Pydantic validation on request bodies
Async DB sessions with commit/rollback

features.title: Agent definitions and tool registry
features.description: 5 versioned agent definitions (planner, designer, code-gen, pr-creator, build-verifier) with schema discovery at phase start
acceptance_criteria: Each agent has model, max_turns, budget, allowed_tools
ToolRegistry.build() returns valid schemas
validate_tool_call() blocks invalid calls

features.title: Agent runner with SDK integration and cost tracking
features.description: run_phase() dispatches via Claude SDK query(), captures session_id, extracts cost_usd/tokens/turns/duration
acceptance_criteria: RunResult includes cost, tokens, session_id, stop_reason
SDK errors mapped to application errors
Cost tracked per run in AgentRun model

features.title: PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks
features.description: Workspace boundary enforcement, bash argv validation (no shell metacharacters), audit logging of all tool calls
acceptance_criteria: File ops outside workspace blocked
Shell metacharacters (|;&`$) blocked
All tool calls logged to agent_run_events

features.title: Deterministic orchestrator with phase routing
features.description: task_status → phase handler dispatch for all SDLC phases: planning, design, implementation, quality_gates, pr_creation, build_verify
acceptance_criteria: Each status maps to exactly one handler
Blocked statuses halt dispatch
Errors mark task FAILED with reason

features.title: Concurrent quality gates with AND aggregation
features.description: Code quality (Ruff/ESLint), testing (pytest/Jest), security (Semgrep 2-pass), dependency (Trivy) — asyncio.gather with per-gate timeouts
acceptance_criteria: Gates run concurrently
One FAIL = overall FAIL
Timeouts produce TIMEOUT status
Tiered scheduling (pre/post integration)

features.title: Gate feedback retry loop
features.description: Gate failure → auto-remediation → agent retry → CAPABILITY_LIMIT after max_retries=2. Dead-letter queue for unrecoverable tasks
acceptance_criteria: Auto-remediation attempted first (semgrep --autofix)
Agent re-dispatched with failure feedback
CAPABILITY_LIMIT after 2 retries
Dead-letter timestamp recorded

features.title: Database models and async session management
features.description: 13 SQLAlchemy models with enums, async session factory, SQLite + PostgreSQL support, init_db() for dev
acceptance_criteria: All 13 tables created on startup
Async sessions with expire_on_commit=False
Both SQLite and PostgreSQL connection strings work

features.title: Git worktree workspace management
features.description: Per-task isolated git worktrees with branch naming convention task/{task_id}
acceptance_criteria: create_workspace() creates worktree + branch
WorkspaceInfo returned with path, branch, is_worktree
Errors raise WorkspaceError

features.title: Harnessability scorer and routing
features.description: 8-point project readiness score: type annotations, linting, tests, module boundaries, API contracts. Routes to PROCEED/ARCHITECT_REVIEW/REJECT
acceptance_criteria: Score 0-8 based on 5 checks
Score < 3 = REJECT
Score 3-4 = ARCHITECT_REVIEW
Score >= 5 = PROCEED

features.title: Pydantic settings with .env support
features.description: Database, agent, gate, harness settings via Pydantic BaseSettings with env prefix. All configurable via environment variables
acceptance_criteria: Settings load from .env file
Each section has env prefix (DB_, AGENT_, GATE_, HARNESS_)
Defaults are sensible for local dev

features.title: Structured logging via structlog
features.description: JSON-formatted structured logging with contextvars, stack info, timestamps. Console renderer in debug mode
acceptance_criteria: Logs output as JSON in production
Console output in debug
stdlib logging redirected to structlog

features.title: Docker Compose with PostgreSQL
features.description: Dockerfile (Python 3.11-slim + git) and docker-compose.yml for agent-builder + PostgreSQL
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up starts both services
Agent builder connects to PostgreSQL
Health check passes

features.title: React SPA dashboard — Board, Metrics, Approval pages
features.description: Vite + React 19 + TypeScript, shadcn/ui Luma preset, GSAP animations, status color system, 3s polling, dark mode
acceptance_criteria: Board shows 5 pipeline sections with task cards
Metrics shows KPI cards + cost chart + runs table
Approval shows thread + gate results + action buttons
Dark mode works
Build passes (<150KB gzipped)

features.title: Typed API client and TypeScript interfaces
features.description: lib/types.ts mirrors Pydantic models, lib/api.ts provides typed fetch wrappers for all 5 dashboard endpoints
acceptance_criteria: All TS types match backend Pydantic schemas
fetchBoard/fetchMetrics/fetchApprovalDetails/dispatchTask/submitApproval typed
Error handling on non-200 responses

features.title: Design language documentation
features.description: docs/design-docs/design-language.md — visual identity, typography, status colors, component conventions, animation principles, anti-patterns
acceptance_criteria: Retrievable via workflow --docs-dir docs summary design-language
Covers all 8 sections
Trigger wired in project CLAUDE.md

features.title: End-to-end pipeline validation with synthetic project
features.description: Create a trivial Python project, dispatch through the full SDLC pipeline (plan → design → implement → gates → PR → done), validate every phase executes correctly with real Claude SDK credentials
acceptance_criteria: Synthetic project created and scored by harnessability
Task dispatched and planner agent runs successfully
At least one quality gate executes and returns pass/fail
Approval flow works from dashboard
Task reaches DONE status
Cost tracking shows real USD values

features.title: Backend tests — API routes and orchestrator
features.description: Test API route contracts (request/response shapes, status codes, error cases) and orchestrator dispatch logic (status transitions, phase routing). Target 80% coverage
acceptance_criteria: test_api_routes.py covers all 16 endpoints
test_orchestrator.py covers dispatch for each TaskStatus
test_gate_feedback.py covers retry loop and CAPABILITY_LIMIT
test_dashboard_api.py covers board/metrics/approval JSON shapes
pytest --cov shows >= 80%

features.title: SSE (Server-Sent Events) for real-time dashboard updates
features.description: Replace 3s polling on Board and Approval pages with SSE endpoint. Backend pushes board state changes and approval thread updates
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/dashboard/events returns SSE stream
Board page reconnects on disconnect
Approval page receives thread updates via SSE
Fallback to polling if SSE connection fails

features.title: Project setup page — create projects and features from UI
features.description: New React page for creating projects (name, repo_url, language) and adding features (title, description, priority). Uses existing POST API endpoints
acceptance_criteria: Project creation form with validation
Feature creation form nested under project
Created project appears on Board immediately
Follows design language (same patterns, no wizard)

features.title: Dead-letter queue management page
features.description: React page showing CAPABILITY_LIMIT tasks with failure context, agent run history, and re-dispatch button. Manual fix + re-dispatch workflow
acceptance_criteria: Lists all tasks with status CAPABILITY_LIMIT or FAILED
Shows failure reason, last agent run details, gate results
Re-dispatch button resets retry_count and re-dispatches task
Follows design language patterns

features.title: Feature filter dropdown on Board page
features.description: Add feature selector at top of Board page to filter tasks by feature. All features shown by default
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown lists all features from current project
Selecting a feature filters pipeline sections to that feature's tasks
'All features' option shows everything
Selection persists across polling refreshes

features.title: Remove Jinja2 legacy dashboard
features.description: Delete dashboard/ module, templates/, static/, HTMX routes. Remove conditional fallback in app.py. React SPA is the only dashboard
acceptance_criteria: dashboard/ directory deleted
No Jinja2 imports remain in codebase
app.py serves React dist/ without fallback
Build still passes

features.title: Feature-level dispatch locking
features.description: asyncio.Lock keyed by feature_id prevents concurrent task dispatch within the same feature. Different features can run in parallel
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent dispatch to same feature serialized
Concurrent dispatch to different features runs in parallel
Lock released on dispatch completion or error

features.title: Git init + Alembic migrations
features.description: Initialize git repository, add Alembic for database schema migrations. Replace init_db() auto-create with migration-based schema management
acceptance_criteria: git init with .gitignore
Alembic configured with async PostgreSQL support
Initial migration generated from current models
alembic upgrade head creates all 13 tables
init_db() only used for SQLite dev mode

features.title: Frontend tests — Vitest + MSW
features.description: Unit tests for React pages using Vitest with MSW for API mocking. Cover data rendering, error states, polling behavior
acceptance_criteria: Vitest configured in vite.config.ts
MSW handlers mock all 5 API endpoints
BoardPage test: renders sections with mocked data
MetricsPage test: renders KPI values
ApprovalPage test: renders thread and action buttons
Error state tests for API failures

features.title: Fix detect-project.sh for src/ layout and pyproject.toml dependencies
features.description: Detection script misses FastAPI in pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]. Fix grep to search dependency sections and check src/**/main.py for entry points
acceptance_criteria: Detects FastAPI from pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]
Finds entry points in src/ layout
Returns correct JSON for this project (type=api, framework=fastapi)

features.title: React error boundaries
features.description: Add error boundary components to catch React render errors. Show user-friendly error state instead of blank screen
acceptance_criteria: Top-level error boundary wraps Routes
Per-page error boundary for each route
Error UI follows design language (destructive indicator pattern)
Retry button re-renders the page

features.title: Security hooks — prompt injection detection, egress monitor, permission store
features.description: Three security/audit features adapted from Goose. (1) Two-layer prompt injection detection: PreToolUse blocks injection-laden tool inputs, PostToolUse detects injection in tool output and injects systemMessage warning. (2) Egress monitor: PostToolUse hook on Bash logs all network destinations (URLs, git remotes, S3, SSH). (3) Permission store: PreToolUse hook checks per-project JSON cache of tool permission decisions with TTL. Also: refactor hooks.py to use correct SDK signatures (PreToolUseHookInput, PostToolUseHookInput, HookContext) and wire hooks to ClaudeAgentOptions.hooks in runner.py
acceptance_criteria: hooks.py refactored to SDK hook signatures (PreToolUseHookInput, tool_use_id, HookContext)
runner.py wires hooks via options.hooks with HookMatcher(matcher=pattern, timeout=30.0)
security/prompt_inspector.py: HIGH patterns block via PreToolUse, MEDIUM/LOW logged via PostToolUse systemMessage
security/egress_monitor.py: extracts URLs, git remotes, S3, SSH, docker push from Bash commands — audit only
security/permission_store.py: JSON-backed store with check/record/cleanup_expired, SHA-256 context hashing
SecurityFinding model added to db/models.py
All hooks wrapped in try/catch (exceptions must not crash query)
Tests: test_prompt_inspector.py, test_egress_monitor.py, test_permission_store.py — happy path + edge cases

features.title: Phase telemetry instrumentation
features.description: Collect per-phase metrics (duration, token usage, context %, cost, outcome) after every agent run. Enables data-driven prioritization of context budget and parallel dispatch features. Place in existing observability/ module alongside logging.py. Add JSON API endpoint and feed data into MetricsPage
acceptance_criteria: observability/phase_telemetry.py: PhaseMetrics dataclass + PhaseTelemetryCollector
Orchestrator._run_agent() records PhaseMetrics after every RunResult
context_pct calculated as tokens_input / model_context_limit
get_phase_summary() returns avg_duration, avg_context_pct, p95_context_pct, avg_cost, failure_rate
GET /api/telemetry/phases returns aggregated phase metrics
Telemetry data persisted (structlog JSON + optional DB)
Tests: test_phase_telemetry.py — recording, aggregation, context % calc

features.title: Context budget enforcement (conditional — requires telemetry data)
features.description: Explicit per-phase token budgets with deterministic head+tail truncation. NO lossy LLM summarization — preserves session chaining contract. Only implement if FT-030 telemetry shows p95_context_pct > 0.70 for any phase. Council-mandated: phase budgets not compaction
acceptance_criteria: agents/context_budget.py: PhaseBudget dataclass with max_input_tokens per phase
check_budget() returns bool, truncate_context_to_budget() does head+tail split
Truncation marker [... context truncated to fit phase budget ...] present in output
Session chaining via resume=session_id still works after truncation
CompactionSettings in config.py with enabled=False default (off until telemetry proves need)
Tests: test_context_budget.py — budget check, truncation preserves head+tail, marker present

features.title: Orchestrator-level parallel dispatch (conditional — requires telemetry data)
features.description: Parallelize implementation phase for multi-file tasks. Orchestrator partitions task by file, dispatches code-gen agents via asyncio.gather with semaphore. All-or-nothing failure semantics. Only implement if FT-030 telemetry shows p95_duration_ms > 120s AND tasks routinely touch 3+ files. Council-mandated: orchestrator owns dispatch, not agents
acceptance_criteria: orchestrator/parallel_dispatch.py: TaskPartition (frozen, exclusive file sets), dispatch_partitions()
partition_task() enforces no file overlap, co-locates tests with source, falls back to single partition
asyncio.Semaphore(max_parallel) limits concurrent agents
All-or-nothing: one partition failure = entire task FAILED
parent_run_id added to AgentRun model for subagent lineage
SubagentSettings in config.py with enabled=False default
ToolRegistry built independently per subagent (tool availability determinism preserved)
Tests: test_parallel_dispatch.py — partitioning, no overlap, all-or-nothing, semaphore

features.title: Recipe system — YAML task templates with parameterization
features.description: Declarative YAML recipes override AgentDefinition fields (prompt additions, lower max_turns/budget). Recipes are additive only — cannot raise budgets, change model, or modify tools (preserves agent-as-artifact + tool_registry invariant). Only implement after 3+ customer workflow patterns identified
acceptance_criteria: recipes/loader.py: Recipe dataclass, load_recipe() from YAML, apply_recipe() returns new AgentDefinition
Recipes can APPEND prompt additions, LOWER max_turns/budget — cannot raise or change model/tools
recipe_name and recipe_params (JSON) columns added to Task model
GET /api/recipes and GET /api/recipes/{name} endpoints
At least one example recipe in recipes/ directory
Tests: test_recipes.py — YAML loading, parameter validation, constraint enforcement (reject model/tool override)
